phase_goal: Prevent Cranfield-specific overfitting: build a BEIR-compatible indexing pipeline, BEIR search/explain endpoints, an evaluation comparison against Cranfield, and a transferability gate that accepts or rejects Phase 1 candidate techniques.

completed_phases.final_deliverable: SLAB-RS article series published at https://feroshjacob.github.io/series/self-learning-agent-based-retail-search/ (Parts 1-4, A-0002/A-0003/A-0004)

pending_approvals: Phase 2 BEIR dataset scope: recommended SciFact + NFCorpus + FiQA-2018; alternatives are small-only (SciFact + NFCorpus) or broad (add TREC-COVID). Ferosh to decide.

Phase 2 transferability gate policy: recommended frozen-config majority rule (Cranfield-frozen hyperparameters, must beat BM25 field-sum baseline on majority of BEIR datasets); alternatives are strict all-datasets or per-dataset tuning. Ferosh to decide.
next_recommended_actions: Before or during Phase 2 planning, refresh the Phase 1 story ledger: update the evolution timeline and .mde/traceability.json article columns (SE-0002 -> A-0003, SE-0003/SE-0004 -> A-0004), mark A-0004 as PUBLISHED with its live URL like A-0003, record published URLs for A-0002/Part 2, and decide whether Part 1 gets a repo artifact ID (A-0001 is unused).

Run Phase 2 Generation 0-style planning: select BEIR datasets, define the transferability gate, and update the validation strategy for BEIR evaluation; extend the SE/ADL/ARCH ledger convention to BEIR experiments so Phase 2 articles can be told from the ledger.
Build the BEIR-compatible indexing pipeline and BEIR search/explain endpoints.
Test ARCH-0.2-candidate refined prf-rerank and ARCH-0.3-candidate remote BGE hybrid for transferability before any public/default promotion.
Compare the simpler remote BGE hybrid path against BGE-feature LTR before accepting LTR complexity.
Add runtime query-vector generation or a bounded known-query embedding cache before enabling live BGE milestone search results.
Tag or record the Phase 1 closing repository state so the article series links to a stable commit.
